arts.ttl: Plymouth Argyle agreement to sign long term target – Player now in England
arts.sum: There’s a claim in the Swedish media that Elfsborg midfielder Michael Baidoo is on the verge signing for Plymouth Argyle. The Ghanian was linked with a move to the Championship side in recent months and January always looked like a prime time for the deal to happen. arts.ttl: Mark Robins makes clear point on Stoke City style in snub to 'boring football'
arts.sum: Sometimes you’ve got to understand if you want to try to control the ball it has to be a certain way but I don’t want to watch ponderous football. He’s a great coach, a great person but seeing Ryan the last two games taking the team and we’ve seeing Stoke City play for the last two games and I think the fans have felt that a little bit as well – well, they’ve definitely felt it.

arts.ttl: How Mark Robins used time away to put together new Stoke City backroom team
arts.sum: He has been joined at Stoke by England under-20s lead coach Paul Nevin and Wales assistant manager James Rowberry, while Alex Morris is staying on as first team coach and Darren Behcet is returning to his role as goalkeeper coach. Sporting director Jon Walters said: “Alex is a Stoke City fan and really helped towards the back end of last year and he’s a great guy to have in there, who Mark will get to know.

arts.ttl: Wayne Rooney's exit forces Plymouth into embarrassing U-turn over lucrative plan
arts.sum: Wayne Rooney's time in charge of Plymouth Argyle lasted just a few months, with the England and Manchester United star leaving his managerial post with the Championship side
